examples.title: Find Roots in Forest
examples.description: Algorithm to find all root nodes in a forest represented using parent pointers
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FindRoots(parent[], n)
// Input: parent array of size n
// Output: List of root indices
BEGIN
 roots = empty list
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 IF parent[i] == -1 THEN
 roots.append(i)
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN roots
END
examples.explanation: Scans the parent array and identifies all indices where parent value is -1, indicating these are root nodes of trees in the forest.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is number of roots

examples.title: Find Depth of Node
examples.description: Algorithm to find depth of any node in a forest by following parent pointers to root
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FindDepth(parent[], node)
// Input: parent array and node index
// Output: Depth of the node
BEGIN
 depth = 0
 current = node
 WHILE parent[current] != -1 DO
 depth = depth + 1
 current = parent[current]
 END WHILE
 RETURN depth
END
examples.explanation: Starting from the given node, traverses parent pointers upward until reaching a root (-1), counting the number of steps.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree

examples.title: Count Trees in Forest
examples.description: Algorithm to count the number of separate trees in a forest
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CountTrees(parent[], n)
// Input: parent array of size n
// Output: Number of trees in forest
BEGIN
 count = 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 IF parent[i] == -1 THEN
 count = count + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN count
END
examples.explanation: Simply counts the number of nodes with parent value -1, as each such node is a root of a tree in the forest.

examples.title: Left-Child Right-Sibling Conversion
examples.description: Algorithm to convert a general tree to binary tree using LCRS representation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LCRS_Convert(root)
// Input: Root of general tree
// Output: Root of equivalent binary tree
BEGIN
 IF root == NULL THEN
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
 
 binaryNode = new Node(root.data)
 binaryNode.left = LCRS_Convert(root.firstChild)
 binaryNode.right = LCRS_Convert(root.nextSibling)
 
 RETURN binaryNode
END
examples.explanation: Recursively converts: first child becomes left child, next sibling becomes right child. This allows representing general trees/forests as binary trees.

examples.title: Forest Traversal
examples.description: Algorithm to traverse all trees in a forest
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TraverseForest(parent[], n)
// Input: Forest represented by parent array
// Output: All nodes visited
BEGIN
 roots = FindRoots(parent, n)
 FOR EACH r IN roots DO
 PreorderTraversal(r)
 END FOR
END

ALGORITHM PreorderTraversal(node)
BEGIN
 IF node == -1 THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 Visit(node)
 children = GetChildren(node)
 FOR EACH c IN children DO
 PreorderTraversal(c)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Finds all roots first, then performs traversal (preorder shown) on each tree separately. Visits all nodes in the forest.
